_provenance.oracle_status: no_canonical (stripped_export — oracle session body is unusable; rubric is the only ground truth, confirmed by oracle_session.jsonl header _status:no_canonical and v2 _task_quality_flags.no_canonical_stub)
_provenance.user_ask_anchor: instruction.md (single user turn): 'investigate whether we can finetune granite docling vlm model with Unsloth, and if not then we should research, investigate and lay out plan to implement this. This should cover all the offering of Unsloth as much as possible (like SFT, DPO, GRPO, etc.)'
_provenance.intent_taxonomy_used: oracle_intents.json (intents 0-21, 21 total). Goals are derived only from intent_kind in {request, correction, question} and from instruction.md verbatim. Context-kind intents (tracebacks, environment facts) are used only to scope which problems the user asked the agent to solve.

_provenance.rationale_against_oracle_leak: Goal text avoids specific file names, class names, identifier names, and HF class names as REQUIRED tokens; where they appear ('e.g. FastIdefics3Model', 'e.g. VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM') they are illustrative examples derived from the user's domain (the user named Unsloth, Idefics3, and the SFT/DPO/GRPO surface in instruction.md; the user pasted requires_grad_pre_hook tracebacks themselves in intents 13-15, 20). The user did NOT name files; goal_2's 'sibling dispatch list' and 'package public surface' are described behaviorally and accept any equivalent location.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission must produce in-repo source changes that actually deliver the user's feature, not effort-only artefacts. Disqualifying patterns: (a) only research/planning/design documents with no functional code; (b) a new module containing only stubs, pass-bodies, or TODO comments; (c) monkey-patching an installed third-party package on disk (site-packages, /usr/lib) as the sole fix, without an in-repo source change that survives reinstall; (d) a new model class that is never wired into the library's own dispatch surface (not registered in the supported-VLM list and not re-exported from the package's public entry point), making it unreachable by the library's own loader; (e) deletions of existing supported-VLM entries, public exports, or unrelated source files that break the library on the unmodified base.
completeness_goals.rationale: Guardrail derived from the instruction.md ask for a real implementation ('research, investigate AND lay out plan to implement') combined with intent_2 ('let's start with option A, I got the time') — the user committed to full implementation, not a doc. The monkey-patch ban traces to intent_18, where the user explicitly pushed back on the agent's local-workaround framing. Anti-effort gate only: cannot earn positive reward; may zero reward on egregious failures.

completeness_goals.goal: A new in-repo source module is added under the library's models package that defines a real, substantive model class for the requested VLM family — a class with at least one method definition or non-trivial base-class inheritance, not an empty placeholder. The class is importable as a top-level symbol of the new module under a stable, conventional name (e.g. a FastIdefics3Model-shaped name following the library's existing FastXxxModel convention). The new module compiles cleanly under py_compile.
completeness_goals.rationale: Primary deliverable. instruction.md asks the agent to 'lay out plan to implement' Unsloth support for the named VLM family, and intents 2/4 commit to executing that plan. Without a real class, no part of the feature exists. Mechanism left open (file path, exact class name, base-class choice) so different but valid designs are not penalized; the only firm constraint is that the class is substantive and importable.

completeness_goals.goal: The new VLM family is wired into the library's own dispatch and public-export surface so that the library can actually reach the new class: an identifier for the new VLM family is added to the library's supported-VLM list (whatever that list happens to be — the agent must locate it), AND the new class is re-exported from the models package's public entry point via a relative import from the new module, AND every pre-existing entry in that supported-VLM list and every pre-existing public export name in the package's public entry point is preserved unchanged.
completeness_goals.rationale: Without registration and export, the new class is dead code from the user's perspective. instruction.md asks for the feature to 'cover all the offering of Unsloth as much as possible' which presupposes the new family is reachable through Unsloth's normal API. The 'preserve existing entries' clause is the in-goal anti-gaming hedge: an agent that achieves the additive side by deleting siblings should not score. This is independent of (and not redundant with) the manifest's separate regression gate; the goal owns it because the user's intent — extending the supported surface, not shrinking it — demands both sides.

completeness_goals.goal: A model-loading entry point on the new class connects to the appropriate Hugging Face conditional-generation / vision-to-seq class for this VLM family, so calling the loader actually instantiates a usable HF model. This can be satisfied by an explicit from_pretrained method body that references the right HF class, OR by inheriting from a shared library base class (a FastVisionModel/FastBaseModel-shaped base) that supplies the loader and dispatches via AutoModelForVision2Seq / AutoModelForImageTextToText, OR by delegation to an existing shared loader utility.
completeness_goals.rationale: Essential for the feature to do anything at runtime: the user actively tested loading on Colab (intents 8-11, 14, 17-20 are all post-load tracebacks the user pasted, which only exist because the user got far enough to load the model). instruction.md's 'cover all the offering of Unsloth' implies the new class must integrate with the library's standard load path. Mechanism left explicitly open across the three accepted approaches so equivalent designs are not penalized.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission addresses the requires_grad pre-hook crash on empty-tuple inputs that this VLM family triggers during forward pass. The fix lives in the project's own source tree (so it survives reinstall) and matches the architectural pattern that supported VLMs already follow — for example by ensuring the new class exposes a working get_input_embeddings so the existing post-hook path is taken (the same path the supported VLMs use), or by widening the library's own peft utilities to handle the empty-inputs case symmetrically with how the other VLMs route. A submission that only narrows the crashing pre-hook ('return early on empty tuple') WITHOUT investigating why the supported VLMs never reach that branch — i.e. without surfacing the pre-hook vs post-hook routing — counts as a workaround, not a proper fix, and is partial credit at best.
completeness_goals.rationale: The session's central debugging arc — 10+ turns of user-pasted tracebacks (intents 13, 14, 15, 20) — culminates in the user's explicit correction (intent_18) that the supported VLMs must already handle this, and the user's explicit question (intent_16) about widening the library to support the new architecture rather than working around it. The 'must match the supported-VLM pattern, not narrowly silence the error' clause is the in-goal anti-gaming hedge: the user explicitly rejected the narrow-silence framing in-session.

completeness_goals.goal: LoRA target-module configuration appropriate for this VLM family is present in the new module — either an explicit list of projection-layer target names (q_proj / k_proj / v_proj / o_proj and the MLP projections gate_proj / up_proj / down_proj or an equivalent subset), or a target_modules keyword wired into a get_peft_model-shaped call, or delegation to an existing shared LoRA-config helper in the library. The configuration must be reachable from the new class (referenced, returned, or otherwise resolvable), not a dangling unused constant.
completeness_goals.rationale: instruction.md is explicit that the deliverable must 'cover all the offering of Unsloth as much as possible (like SFT, DPO, GRPO, etc.)'. All three named offerings depend on PEFT-style fine-tuning, which is meaningless without target-module configuration. Without LoRA targets the class compiles but is unusable for the workflows the user named. Mechanism left open.

decomposition_notes: Five positively-weighted goals (goal_1..goal_5) sum to 1.00 (0.26 + 0.26 + 0.18 + 0.16 + 0.14). goal_0 is an anti-effort guardrail at weight 0.0. Weights are an INDEPENDENT, intent-salience judgment and are deliberately NOT mirrored from test_manifest.yaml's F2P gate weights (the manifest mixes inner-test and upstream-build gates under a weighted-replace algebra; the rubric measures user-intent coverage). The two core wiring goals (1+2) take the largest share because they are the load-bearing pair without which 'investigate feasibility / implement option A' (instruction.md, intent_1, intent_2) produces nothing reachable; goal_3 is core-but-smaller because it often comes free with the right inheritance choice; goal_4 sits in secondary at elevated weight because it is the user's longest-debated point but it is also the one most plausibly delivered in a partial form; goal_5 is the smallest positive weight because it is the most mechanical of the asks while still being directly anchored in instruction.md.

checks_run: provenance_per_goal: every positively-weighted goal cites at least one user-derived source (instruction.md OR an oracle_intents.json intent_kind in {request, correction, question}) — PASS

no_oracle_leak: no goal REQUIRES a specific file path, class name, or identifier that the user did not themselves name; HF class names appear only as 'e.g.' examples and the user themselves named the Idefics3 architecture in instruction.md — PASS

coverage_of_user_asks: every request/correction/question intent maps to a goal OR is documented as out-of-scope in _provenance.user_asks_covered_table (intents 9, 10, 12, 19 documented as notebook-side / docs-side, not in-repo deliverables) — PASS
goal_0_present_zero_weight: anti-effort goal_0 exists, tier=anti_effort, weight=0.0 — PASS

anti_gaming_in_goal_text: goal_0 enumerates effort-only failure modes; goal_2 carries the 'preserve existing entries' clause inline; goal_4 carries the 'narrow silence is not a proper fix' clause inline; goal_5 requires the config to be reachable, not dangling — PASS

weights_independent_of_F2P: rubric weights (0.26/0.26/0.18/0.16/0.14) are explicitly different from the manifest's per-gate weights (0.09 x 5 + 0.06 + 0.09 + 0.20 + 0.20 upstream) and decomposition_notes documents why — PASS
mechanism_left_open_where_user_left_it_open: goals 1, 3, 4, 5 each enumerate at least two acceptable approaches; goal_2 leaves the list-name and entry-point location to the agent — PASS

no_redundancy_with_P2P_gate_phrasing: goal_2's 'preserve existing entries' is the rubric's own anti-deletion clause, justified independently by the user's intent to extend (not shrink) the supported surface; it overlaps with manifest P2P gates but is not derived from them — PASS
rewrites_after_critique: v2's goal_2 phrasing said 'e.g. an entry added to the VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM list in vision.py' which is closer to an oracle identifier than to user-derived prose. v3 rephrased to 'whatever that list happens to be — the agent must locate it' to move the locating burden onto the agent and keep the only firm constraint behavioral.

v2's goal_4 mixed the in-repo-not-site-packages constraint with the architectural-match constraint. v3 splits the in-repo-not-site-packages part out into goal_0 (effort-only / monkey-patch ban) and keeps goal_4 focused on the architectural-match criterion the user actually raised in intent_18.
v1's flat references to F2P test numbers in rationales were removed entirely — rationales now cite only instruction.md and oracle_intents intent_ids.

_dropped_from_v2.v3_change: softened to behavioral: 'the library's supported-VLM list' without naming vision.py or VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM as required identifiers; the names appear only in goal_2's example-bearing phrasing and only because the user described the library by name in instruction.md.
_dropped_from_v2.reason: v2 named the exact file (vision.py) and exact list (VLLM_SUPPORTED_VLM); the user never named these in instruction.md or in any request/correction/question intent. Naming them in the rubric crosses into oracle-leakage.

_dropped_from_v2.v3_change: split: monkey-patch ban moved to goal_0 (effort gate); goal_4 retained the architectural-match-with-supported-VLMs criterion which directly traces to intent_18.
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Cleaner trace from each goal to a specific user ask.

_dropped_from_v2.v3_change: rationales no longer mention 'F2P test N'; they cite instruction.md and intent_ids only.
_dropped_from_v2.reason: Rubric goals are independent of the test manifest by construction; v2 rationales blurred that line.

_added_for_coverage.coverage_added: Explicit enumeration of (a) doc-only submissions, (b) stub/TODO submissions, (c) site-packages-only monkey-patch submissions, (d) unwired-module submissions, (e) destructive-edit submissions. v2 covered (a)-(d) but did not enumerate (e); the destructive-edit case is added because goal_2 now relies on goal_0 to enforce the symmetric 'don't break the unmodified base' floor.
_added_for_coverage.user_ask_anchor: instruction.md commits the agent to a real implementation; intent_2 ('I got the time') closes the 'we'll just plan it' escape hatch.

_added_for_coverage.coverage_added: Added 'must be reachable from the new class — not a dangling unused constant'. v2 accepted presence anywhere in the module.
_added_for_coverage.user_ask_anchor: instruction.md 'cover all the offering of Unsloth as much as possible (like SFT, DPO, GRPO, etc.)' — a constant the new class can't see does not deliver any of those offerings.
